title: Add graceful shutdown with preStop hook and startup probe
summary: Adds preStop lifecycle hook (sleep 5), terminationGracePeriodSeconds (30), and startup probe (httpGet /v3:5000, failureThreshold 30, periodSeconds 10) to the keystone-api container in buildKeystoneDeployment. Existing liveness (TCPSocket) and readiness (HTTPGet) probes are unchanged. Unit tests in reconcile_deployment_test.go verify all three new fields plus probe non-regression. Integration test TestIntegration_GracefulShutdownSpec verifies the fields survive a full envtest reconciliation cycle.

review_steps.1_INTENT: Plan specifies adding preStop hook, terminationGracePeriodSeconds, and startup probe to eliminate two classes of transient errors during rollouts: (a) race between endpoint removal and SIGTERM, (b) liveness-kill loops during slow cold starts.
review_steps.2_SCOPE: Changes limited to reconcile_deployment.go (+19 lines), reconcile_deployment_test.go (+22 lines), integration_test.go (+57 lines), and progress JSON. No scope creep.
review_steps.3_VERIFICATION: go vet: clean. Unit tests: 24/24 PASS. golangci-lint: clean (1 pre-existing staticcheck warning in unrelated reconcile_networkpolicy_test.go).
review_steps.4_PATTERNS: Uses ptr.To(int64(30)) consistent with existing ptr.To usage in security_context.go and reconcile_trustflush.go. Uses intstr.FromInt32(5000) consistent with existing probe definitions. Integration test follows established TestIntegration_PDBSpec pattern (SkipIfEnvTestUnavailable, setupEnvTestWithController, createPrerequisites, integrationBrownfieldKeystone, driveFullReconciliation, fetch+assert). Gomega assertions via NewGomegaWithT(t).
review_steps.5_TESTS: Unit test verifies all three new fields with exact value assertions. Existing liveness (TCPSocket:5000, initialDelay=15, period=20) and readiness (HTTPGet /v3:5000, initialDelay=5, period=10) probe assertions still pass unchanged. Integration test verifies all three fields survive API server round-trip. Comments reference CC-0063 and REQ IDs.
review_steps.6_SECURITY: No security concerns. preStop hook runs 'sleep 5' — no user input, no secrets, no external calls.
review_steps.7_COMPLEXITY: Minimal: three new struct fields in the deployment builder, corresponding test assertions. No abstractions, no helpers, no configuration. Simplest possible implementation.
review_steps.8_COMPLETENESS: All 6 acceptance criteria met. All 3 tasks (1.1, 1.2, 2.1) implemented.

tests_checklist.item: Unit tests exist and pass for all three new fields
Existing liveness probe assertions pass unchanged
Existing readiness probe assertions pass unchanged
startupProbe uses httpGet (not tcpSocket or exec)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (ptr.To, intstr.FromInt32, probe structure)
Clear naming and comments with CC-0063 and REQ references
No dead code or unnecessary abstractions

security_checklist.item: No user inputs to validate (preStop is a static sleep command)
No external calls or network operations in lifecycle hook

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible — three struct fields added
EnsureDeployment uses unconditional spec updates, so no normalization needed for new fields
Integration test follows established envtest pattern

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No future-proofing or configurability beyond what was requested

fail_fast_checklist.item: Not applicable — no error paths introduced (static configuration only)

defensive_checklist.item: Not applicable — no external inputs (static lifecycle configuration)

suggested_improvements: D2 [AUTO_FIX]: operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go:1733-1739 — The manual container-finding loop duplicates the existing findContainerByName helper in security_context_test.go (same package). Consider using: container := findContainerByName(deploy.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers, "keystone-api"). This is a minor consistency suggestion only.
